qa.a: ALIBI (Logged & Verified) is a kitchen inventory system built around accountability: every quantity change is logged, time-stamped, and reviewable — so ‘we had it yesterday’ becomes a measurable history.

qa.q: What does “Logged & Verified” mean in practice?
qa.a: It means changes don’t just mutate a number — they create an event (received/used/moved/count) that can be confirmed, audited, and rolled back mentally when reconciling inventory.

qa.q: How do I get value fast?
qa.a: Start with your top 20 high‑variance items (meat, dairy, disposables). Log every receive/use/count for a week. The history will immediately show where drift happens.
Common causes: cleared browser storage, a new browser profile/device, or pointing to a different backend environment. Check localStorage/IndexedDB keys or API base URL.
A stock‑count workflow: “count mode” that records a physical count event, explains the delta, and updates inventory while preserving the audit trail.
